sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Topics 37:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Topics 37:1
items.html: The previous incorporation of the female in the male, which was in the first coupling, which equalized them together, caused the male to be incorporated later in the female in the second coupling. (Item 11)
